title: Log Authentication Attempts and Account State Changes
description: The system shall log all authentication attempts (success/failure) and account state changes (lock/unlock) with details including timestamp, username, IP address, action type, and outcome.
rationale: To enable security auditors to review and audit security events for compliance and threat detection.
business_value: Improves the system's ability to detect unauthorized access attempts and account compromises, enhancing overall security posture.

source.description: As a security auditor I need the system to log all authentication attempts (success/failure) and account state changes (lock/unlock) so that I can review and audit security events for compliance and threat detection.

acceptance_criteria: Given the system is operational When a user attempts authentication (success or failure) Then the system records the event with details (timestamp, username, IP, outcome)
Given the system is operational When an account is locked or unlocked Then the system records the state change with details (timestamp, username, action)
postconditions: Authentication attempts and account state changes are logged with required details
Logs are stored in a secure, tamper-evident format
functional_constraints: Logs include timestamp, username, IP address, action type, and outcome for authentication attempts and lock/unlock events
security_requirements: Logs must be stored in a secure, tamper-evident format
edge_cases: What happens if the system is under heavy load during authentication attempts?
How should the system handle failed log storage operations?
What are the consequences of an attacker attempting to manipulate log data?

interfaces.name: Logging Database
interfaces.description: The system interacts with a secure, tamper-evident database to store authentication and account state change logs.

interfaces.name: Central Logging Service
interfaces.description: Logs are sent to a central logging service for unified management and analysis of security events.

test_cases.description: Verify that successful authentication attempts are logged with correct details.
test_cases.expected_result: The system logs the event with timestamp, username, IP address, and outcome as 'Success'.

test_cases.description: Verify that failed authentication attempts are logged with correct details.
test_cases.expected_result: The system logs the event with timestamp, username, IP address, and outcome as 'Failure'.

test_cases.description: Verify that account lock events are logged with correct details.
test_cases.expected_result: The system logs the state change with timestamp, username, and action as 'Account Locked'.

test_cases.description: Verify that account unlock events are logged with correct details.
test_cases.expected_result: The system logs the state change with timestamp, username, and action as 'Account Unlocked'.
